name: ExtensionAuditEvent_BasedOn
title: R5: Workflow authorization within which this event occurred (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `AuditEvent.basedOn` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`AuditEvent.basedOn` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/CarePlan,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DeviceRequest,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ImmunizationRecommendation,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/MedicationRequest,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/NutritionOrder,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ServiceRequest,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Task)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `AuditEvent.basedOn` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/CarePlan,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/DeviceRequest,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ImmunizationRecommendation,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/MedicationRequest,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/NutritionOrder,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ServiceRequest,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Task)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `AuditEvent.basedOn` has a context of AuditEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.basedOn` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Workflow authorization within which this event occurred
element.definition: Allows tracing of authorizatino for the events and tracking whether proposals/recommendations were acted upon.
